summary: Well-structured shared-meta blueprint that establishes a comprehensive, fair evaluation standard for Agentish files. Defines clean taxonomies (7 file classes, 3 evaluation modes, 10 quality dimensions) with explicit fairness rules preventing common evaluation biases. One undefined actor (Reviewer) and some structural redundancy in .contains() and .defines() blocks are the main issues.
strengths: Explicit fairness rules that separate obsolescence from intrinsic quality, require fresh context, and prevent cross-file contamination — these are the file's highest-value contribution
Complete self-description: every term used in judgment is defined inline, making the file fully recoverable from itself plus the language root
When/then causal chains for special cases (blueprint-state, process-guide, missing live-use evidence) are precise and actionable
Clean three-axis taxonomy (FileClass × Mode × Dimension) with explicit scoping of which modes apply to which classes and which dimensions each mode judges
Actively in use as the governing standard for corpus-wide evaluation, confirming strong live-use evidence
weaknesses: Reviewer actor used in a when() clause but never declared with define.actor() — breaks abstraction discipline and adds interpretive slack
The .contains() enumeration re-lists every entity already declared above, adding ~20 lines of pure structural overhead with zero new semantic content
Several .defines() entries restate what the entity names already convey (e.g., TargetAgentishFile means one repository Agentish artifact under evaluation) — mild density loss
No self-versioning or change-tracking mechanism for the evaluation standard itself, which matters for a shared-meta file that other processes depend on
